annotations.label: Algebraic number

anchor.snippet: an algebraic number is a number that is a root of a non-zero polynomial in one variable with integer

annotations.note: `IsAlgebraic R x` is defined as `∃ p : R[X], p ≠ 0 ∧ aeval x p = 0`; an algebraic number is the instance `IsAlgebraic ℚ (x : ℂ)`.

annotations.label: Golden ratio is algebraic

anchor.snippet: is an algebraic number, because it is a root of the polynomial

annotations.note: `Real.goldenRatio` is defined and `goldenRatio_sq : φ^2 = φ + 1` shows it is a root of X²-X-1, but no explicit `IsAlgebraic` statement exists.

annotations.label: Algebraic numbers include integers, rationals, roots

anchor.snippet: Algebraic numbers include all integers

annotations.note: `isAlgebraic_int`/`isAlgebraic_rat` give integers and rationals are algebraic, and `isAlgebraic_of_mem_rootSet` covers polynomial roots like nth roots.

annotations.label: Algebraic complex numbers form a field

anchor.snippet: Algebraic complex numbers are closed under addition, subtraction, multiplication and division, and hence form a field

annotations.note: `algebraicClosure ℚ ℂ` is the `IntermediateField` of algebraic elements, which is by construction a field.

annotations.label: Algebraic real numbers form a field

anchor.snippet: is also a field

annotations.note: `algebraicClosure ℚ ℝ` is the `IntermediateField` of real algebraic numbers, which is a field.

annotations.label: Transcendental number

anchor.snippet: Numbers which are not algebraic are called transcendental

annotations.note: `Transcendental R x` is defined as `¬ IsAlgebraic R x`.

annotations.label: Almost all numbers are transcendental

anchor.snippet: There are countably infinite algebraic numbers, hence almost all real (or complex) numbers

annotations.note: Countability of the algebraic numbers is formalized as `Algebraic.countable`, but the measure-theoretic 'almost all are transcendental' conclusion is not stated.

annotations.label: All rational numbers are algebraic

anchor.snippet: All rational numbers are algebraic.

annotations.note: `isAlgebraic_rat` states every rational number is algebraic over a field.

annotations.label: Quadratic irrationals are algebraic

anchor.snippet: Quadratic irrational numbers , irrational solutions of a quadratic polynomial

annotations.note: Quadratic irrationals are a special case of `IsAlgebraic`, but there is no decl naming or asserting algebraicity of quadratic irrationals specifically.

annotations.label: Gaussian integers are quadratic integers

anchor.snippet: Gaussian integers , complex numbers a + bi for which both a and b are integers, are also quadratic integers.

annotations.note: `GaussianInt` is defined as `Zsqrtd (-1)`, i.e. the quadratic integer ring ℤ[i].

annotations.label: Constructible numbers

anchor.snippet: A constructible number can be constructed from a given unit length using a straightedge and compass

annotations.note: No straightedge-and-compass constructible number field is formalized (Mathlib's 'Constructible' refers to constructible sets in topology/spectra).

annotations.label: Radical expressions of algebraic numbers

anchor.snippet: Any expression formed from algebraic numbers using any finite combination of the basic arithmetic operations

annotations.note: Algebraic elements form a subalgebra (`Subalgebra.algebraicClosure`) and `IsAlgebraic.of_pow` keeps nth roots algebraic, but no single decl covers arbitrary radical expressions.

annotations.label: Non-radical polynomial roots

anchor.snippet: Polynomial roots that cannot be expressed in terms of the basic arithmetic operations and extraction of n th roots

annotations.note: Such roots are algebraic by definition, but there is no decl asserting that non-solvable polynomial roots are algebraic examples.

annotations.label: Trig values at rational multiples of pi

anchor.snippet: Values of trigonometric functions of rational multiples of π

annotations.note: `isAlgebraic_cos_rat_mul_pi`, `isAlgebraic_sin_rat_mul_pi`, and `isAlgebraic_tan_rat_mul_pi` prove trig values at rational multiples of π are algebraic over ℤ.

annotations.label: Some irrationals are algebraic

anchor.snippet: Some but not all irrational numbers are algebraic

annotations.note: Both `Irrational` and `IsAlgebraic` exist (e.g. irrational √2 is algebraic), but no combined example decl states this.

annotations.label: Golden ratio is algebraic

anchor.snippet: The golden ratio φ is algebraic since it is a root of the polynomial

annotations.note: `goldenRatio_sq : φ^2 = φ + 1` shows φ is a root of X²-X-1, but algebraicity is not stated as an `IsAlgebraic` fact.

annotations.label: Pi and e are not algebraic

anchor.snippet: The numbers π and e are not algebraic numbers

annotations.note: Transcendence of π and e is not in Mathlib (only the analytic part of Lindemann–Weierstrass and transcendence of Liouville numbers are present).

annotations.label: Integer vs rational coefficients equivalence

anchor.snippet: If a polynomial with rational coefficients is multiplied through by the least common denominator

annotations.note: `IsFractionRing.isAlgebraic_iff` gives `IsAlgebraic A x ↔ IsAlgebraic K x` for K the fraction field of A, i.e. ℤ-coefficients vs ℚ-coefficients agree.

annotations.label: Minimal polynomial and degree

anchor.snippet: Given an algebraic number, there is a unique monic polynomial with rational coefficients of least degree

annotations.note: `minpoly R x` is the minimal polynomial, with `minpoly.monic`, `minpoly.min`, and `minpoly.unique` giving the monic least-degree characterization.

annotations.label: Algebraic numbers are dense in the reals

anchor.snippet: The algebraic numbers are dense in the reals .

annotations.note: No decl states that the algebraic real numbers are dense in ℝ.

annotations.label: Algebraic numbers are countable

anchor.snippet: The set of algebraic numbers is countable

annotations.note: `Algebraic.countable` proves `{x | IsAlgebraic R x}` is countable when R is countable.

annotations.label: Algebraic numbers are computable

anchor.snippet: All algebraic numbers are computable and therefore definable and arithmetical .

annotations.note: No decl establishes computability/definability of algebraic numbers.

annotations.label: a + bi algebraic iff a and b algebraic

anchor.snippet: the complex number a + bi is algebraic if and only if both a and b are algebraic

annotations.note: No decl relates algebraicity of a complex number to that of its real and imaginary parts.

annotations.label: Finite degree criterion for algebraicity

anchor.section: Degree of simple extensions of the rationals as a criterion to algebraicity
anchor.snippet: the simple extension of the rationals by

annotations.note: `adjoin.finiteDimensional` shows K⟮x⟯ is finite-dimensional when x is algebraic, with the converse given by `Algebra.IsAlgebraic.of_finite`.

annotations.label: Finite degree of multi-element extension

anchor.section: Degree of simple extensions of the rationals as a criterion to algebraicity
anchor.snippet: It can similarly be proven that for any finite set of algebraic numbers

annotations.note: `finiteDimensional_adjoin` proves K-adjoining a finite set of integral/algebraic elements gives a finite-dimensional extension.

annotations.label: Algebraic numbers closed under arithmetic

anchor.snippet: The sum, difference, product, and quotient (if the denominator is nonzero) of two algebraic numbers is again algebraic

annotations.note: `Subalgebra.algebraicClosure` bundles closure under +, -, * (via `IsAlgebraic.add`/`mul`), and `IsAlgebraic.inv` gives closure under division.

annotations.label: Algebraic numbers form a field

anchor.snippet: Algebraic numbers thus form a field

annotations.note: `algebraicClosure F E` is an `IntermediateField` (hence a field); `Subalgebra.isField_of_algebraic` gives the same for the subalgebra form.

annotations.label: Field of algebraic numbers is algebraically closed

anchor.snippet: Every root of a polynomial equation whose coefficients are algebraic numbers is again algebraic.

annotations.note: When E is algebraically closed (e.g. ℂ), `algebraicClosure.isAlgClosure` shows `algebraicClosure F E` is an algebraic closure of F, hence algebraically closed.

annotations.label: Numbers built from radicals are algebraic

anchor.snippet: Any number that can be obtained from the integers using a finite number of additions

annotations.note: Field-operation closure plus `IsAlgebraic.of_pow` (nth roots stay algebraic) implies radical expressions are algebraic, but no decl asserts this for radical towers directly.

annotations.label: Elementary / closed-form numbers

anchor.snippet: in terms of polynomials, exponentials, and logarithms are called

annotations.note: No notion of elementary/closed-form numbers is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Algebraic integer

anchor.snippet: An algebraic integer is an algebraic number that is a root of a polynomial with integer coefficients with leading coefficient 1

annotations.note: `IsIntegral R x` means x is a root of a monic polynomial over R; an algebraic integer is `IsIntegral ℤ x`.

annotations.label: Algebraic integers form a ring

anchor.snippet: The sum, difference and product of algebraic integers are again algebraic integers, which means that the algebraic integers form a ring

annotations.note: `integralClosure R A` is defined as the `Subalgebra` of integral elements, so `integralClosure ℤ ℂ` is the ring of algebraic integers.

annotations.label: Ring of integers of a number field

anchor.snippet: If K is a number field, its ring of integers is the subring of algebraic integers in K

annotations.note: `NumberField.RingOfIntegers K` is defined as `integralClosure ℤ K`, the algebraic integers of the number field K.
